title: Chironian
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Species
|image=[[File:Njo_lusa.jpg]]

{{Quote|They cut off your horns to make you ugly. They cut off your horns to make you obey. But my horns haven't come through the velvet yet. If they cut the velvet, I'll die!|Lusa|<ref name="TCS" />}}
The '''Chironians''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] distinguished by their [[centauriform]] anatomy; while their torso, [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and head resembled those of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], their lower body was that of a four-legged [[Equine/Legends|equine]]. Another distinctive Chironian feature was the two transparent, smooth horns grown each [[Standard year/Legends|year]] from the forehead. These horns started as small velvet-covered knobs, which eventually broke through as fully-fledged horns. If the velvet was cut prematurely, the wound could prove [[Death/Legends|fatal]]. At least some [[Youngling/Legends|juvenile]] Chironians were red-gold in [[Color/Legends|color]] with white spots on their flanks and back; this color darkened with age and the spots faded. Chironians could grow long [[Hair/Legends|manes]] and tails.

The Chironian species was native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chiron (planet)|Chiron]]. This world was located in a portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] known as the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Ash Worlds]] following the sacking and [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombardment]] of many of its [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] by forces of the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species, circa [[24,500 BBY]]. Thousands of years later, many of the Ash Worlds were interdicted for weapons testing by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. After the collapse of the Empire, at least one Chironian, [[Lusa]], was kidnapped by the [[Empire Reborn]] movement, which sought to revive the Empire under the leadership of [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]]. Lusa was eventually rescued and reunited with her family. For a [[Time/Legends|time]], she joined the radical, anti-[[Human/Legends|Human]], [[Diversity Alliance]], until she quit after being exposed to the Alliance's violent tendencies. Lusa sought help from [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and aided the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in suppressing the Alliance. She later became a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] in the [[New Jedi Order]], and was [[Death/Legends|killed]] while on her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] during the brutal [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] of [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|We're looking for Lusa. She has four feet, not two! She's red-gold, with white spots, and she has horns.|Jaina Solo describes Lusa|<ref name="TCS" />}}
The Chironians were a warm-[[Blood/Legends|blooded]], [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with [[centauriform]] anatomy;<ref name="TCS" /> that is, they had the head and torso of a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] and the lower body of an [[Equine/Legends|equine]] animal. The Chironian lower body had four [[Limb/Legends|legs]],<ref name="WWNJO" /> similar to that of a [[Horse/Legends|horse]],<ref name="YJK9" /> and each ended in a dainty, cloven hoof.<ref name="TCS" /> A Chironian could sit down by folding his or her horse-like legs underneath the body. Chironians were tall, and even while sitting in this manner, they could reach the height of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] adult.<ref name="YJK9" /> A Chironian could also kick backward with both hind legs; this strong kick was sufficient to knock over other beings. A Chironian's pace could vary between a walk, a canter, and a gallop.<ref name="TCS" />

At least some young Chironian had red-gold [[Color/Legends|colored]] bodies,<ref name="TCS" /> with white spots on the flanks and back.<ref name="TCS" /> As Chironians aged, the body color darkened to a rich reddish-brown, like polished [[Cinnamon/Legends|cinnamon]], and the dappled markings on the flanks faded away.<ref name="YJK9" /> Chironians grew long tails, which they often flicked.<ref name="TCS" /> The Chironian torso had two arms, which ended in dexterous hands capable of skipping stones across [[Water/Legends|water]], administering field medicine,<ref name="YJK10">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Bounty]]''</ref> and wielding weapons such as [[Stun-staff|stun sticks]].<ref name="YJK14">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]''</ref>

The Chironian head had two<ref name="WWNJO" /> wide, round [[Eye/Legends|eyes]],<ref name="YJK9" /> which could be red-gold in color,<ref name="TCS" /> and could weep.<ref name="YJK10" /> The eyes were lidded<ref name="TCS" /> and had eyebrows.<ref name="WWNJO" /> The Chironian head had a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] with two nostrils, and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with full lips.<ref name="WWNJO" /> Chironians grew long, curly [[Hair/Legends|manes]] on their heads,<ref name="TCS" /> hair which was sometimes grown to nearly waist-length<ref name="YJK9" /> and flowed down their backs.<ref name="YJK13">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Trouble on Cloud City]]''</ref> Chironian manes darkened with age to a rich reddish-brown color.<ref name="YJK9" />

Each [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|Lusa}}</ref> the Chironian head grew two horns above the temples. These horns started as small knobs covered by red-furred velvet;<ref name="TCS" /> a vascular tissue.<ref name="SWE" /> Once the knobs had grown sufficiently, they broke through the velvet naturally, emerging as transparent horns, as bright as [[Diamond/Legends|diamond]], cool to the touch and with smooth ridges.<ref name="TCS" /> However, if the velvet was cut artificially, Chironians could [[Death/Legends|die]] from the injury.<ref name="TCS" /> Over [[Time/Legends|time]], the horns grew long and sharp enough that adolescent Chironians could use them to gore opponents.<ref name="YJK10" />

Chironians were strong enough to carry a Human on their backs. Chironians produced a scent that, to Humans at least, smelled of [[Tree/Legends|woods]] and [[Spice/Legends|spices]].<ref name="YJK10" /> The species had at least one [[Gender/Legends|sex]], females, and at least some Chironians had [[Force-sensitive/Legends|the capacity]] to use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="TCS" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Jaina! Isn't this fun? It's fun to kick Proctors.|Lusa|<ref name="TCS" />}}
Chironians expressed emotions through the mannerisms of their hooves: when impatient, they stamped them;<ref name="YJK10" /> when excited, they tapped them in a quick dance, or cantered in a spot.<ref name="TCS" /> The centauriforms frequently shook their heads and manes, and when angry, their faces flushed a deeper shade.<ref name="YJK10" /> Young Chironians, when frightened, were known to rub or press their velvet-covered horns against the foreheads of other beings to whom they were emotionally close.<ref name="TCS" /> Chironians could swim, and shook themselves dry after such an exertion.<ref name="YJK9" />

Chironians had expressive,<ref name="TCS" /> husky voices,<ref name="YJK9" /> and could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], although some spoke the [[Language/Legends|language]] with a heavy accent.<ref name="TCS" /> At least some young Chironians, such as [[Lusa]], a Force-sensitive member of the species, wore no clothing on their bodies, shoulders, torsos or midriffs.<ref name="YJK10" /> In her adulthood, however, Lusa wore a robe on her torso.<ref name="WWNJO" />

==History==
{{Quote|She was supposed to be safe. Chiron is a long way from the Yuuzhan Vong.|Jaina Solo, on the death of Lusa|<ref name="SBS8" />}}
The Chironian species was native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Chiron (planet)|Chiron]].<ref name="SBS8" /> Chiron was located in the galactic east of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]],<ref name="Atlas208">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' reveals the location of Chiron within galactic coordinate T-7. Using this as a basis, Chiron's location can be calculated on other maps that do not specifically name the [[Star system/Legends|star system]].</ref> and fell within the borders of the [[Ash Worlds/Legends|Kiirium Reaches]], a portion of the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] that was incorporated into [[Xim/Legends|Xim]] the Despot's [[Xim's empire|empire]] as early as [[25,127 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> Legends related that, at the order of [[Kossak Inijic Ar'durv|Kossak]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], many worlds in the region surrounding the Chironians' homeworld were [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarded]] and [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] [[24,500 BBY|several hundred years]] later so as to create a desolate buffer between the remains of Xim's empire and [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]] to the galactic south. From this destruction, the Chironians' home [[Sector/Legends|sector]],<ref name="Xim">{{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-extra-the-history-of-xim-and-the-tion-cluster|text=Essential Atlas Extra: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster|int=Xim Week: The History of Xim and the Tion Cluster}}</ref> which they shared with the [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssins]] and the [[Unidentified Nyny species|Nyny natives]],<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> came to be known as the Ash Worlds.<ref name="Xim" />
[[File:MonCalamariSpace-TEA.jpg|thumb|left|Chiron and surrounding star systems]]
It was not until [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] that Chiron fell within territory widely explored by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the next major galactic power, although during the [[Jedi Civil War]] of [[3959 BBY]] to [[3956 BBY]], the Chironians' planet fell within [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. Chiron was in unaligned territory during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]];<ref name="TEA" /> however, after the Republic transitioned into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], many of the Ash Worlds were interdicted and used for weapons testing and other shadowy activities.<ref name="Xim" />

Following the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Chiron fell under its polity.<ref name="TEA" /> During this time, at least one Chironian was kidnapped as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] by the [[Empire Reborn]], a secretive movement that sought to recreate the Empire following its defeat by the New Republic.<ref name="TCS" /> Many years later, during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], a conflict between the New Republic and the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a species of [[Yuuzhan Vong galaxy|extra-galactic]] invaders,<ref name="NJOSB">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref> Chiron was thought to be beyond the reach of the aggressors. However, the Yuuzhan Vong succeeded in sending to their world a pack of [[voxyn]]<ref name="SBS8" />—creatures [[Shaper caste|shaped]] by the Yuuzhan Vong to be naturally inclined to hunt and [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].<ref name="NJOSB" /> The voxyn beset the Chironian [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Lusa while she was running through a meadow and killed the woman.<ref name="SBS8" />

By [[137 ABY]], following the ascendency of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]] against the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]—the New Republic's successor state—the Chironians' homeworld fell within one of only a few areas of the galaxy still under the influence of the Galactic Alliance.<ref name="TEA" />

==Chironians in the galaxy==
{{Quote|I had never stopped to think exactly what I was willing to do for the cause I believed in. Was I willing to kill? And if so, what must the victim's crime be to deserve death? Should I judge each one, or could I trust my leader to judge them for me?|Lusa commenting on her experiences with the Diversity Alliance|<ref name="YJK9" />}}
[[File:Lord_Hethrir.png|thumb|right|Lord Hethrir sold Lusa into slavery.]]
In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], the Chironian Lusa was kidnapped from her family on Chiron at a young age<ref name="YJK9" /> and pressed into service by the Empire Reborn.<ref name="TCS" /> Lusa and many other children of diverse species were taken to a [[worldcraft]],<ref name="TCS" /> the ''[[Rebirth]]'',<ref name="AitE">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/97/indexp4.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 2: To a Traitor Go the Spoils}}</ref> owned by the leader of the Empire Reborn, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]]. There, Hethrir ostensibly tested Lusa and the other children for Force-sensitivity; however, Hethrir only selected Human children as Force-sensitives in order to reflect the [[High Human Culture/Legends|Humanocentric]] vision of the Empire. The rest of the children were to be sold as [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]] to his loyal supporters.<ref name="TCS" />

While on the worldcraft, the Chironian became friends with [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], the kidnapped child of the New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]],<ref name="TCS" /><ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|267|Lusa}}</ref> before Lusa was eventually sold to a [[Lusa's owner|wealthy backer]] of the movement. Lusa was taken by her new owner to the [[Crseih Station|Crseih space station]] to witness a decisive ritual to advance the Empire Reborn. However, this plot was [[Confrontation on Crseih Station|foiled]] and the Chironian girl freed through the intervention of Organa Solo, her husband [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]].<ref name="TCS" />

Lusa was returned to her family on Chiron; however, the anger and trauma from her experience never passed, and, in [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], she joined the [[Diversity Alliance]], a fervent anti-Human organization. Over the course of two years, she became radicalized, and carried out various missions for the Alliance, until asked to kill the crew of a [[Scout ship/Legends|scout vessel]]. Backing out of the mission, Lusa sought help from Master Skywalker on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]]. He invited her to spend some time at his [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], where the Chironian reunited with her childhood friend Jaina.<ref name="YJK9" /> Lusa's experience with the Diversity Alliance ultimately contributed to its defeat: she reported to the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] on its activities and accompanied a fact-finding mission to its headquarters on [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]]. Lusa went on to [[Jedi training/Legends|train]] at the Jedi Praxeum,<ref name="YJK11">''[[Young Jedi Knights: The Emperor's Plague]]''</ref> and attained the rank of Jedi Knight.<ref name="CSWE" /> She became closely involved with fellow Jedi Knight [[Raynar Thul]],<ref name="NJOSB" /> but in [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], during the course of the Yuuzhan Vong War, she visited her homeworld, where she was killed by a pack of voxyn.<ref name="SBS8" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[Vonda N. McIntyre]] created the Chironian species along with the Chironian character Lusa for ''[[The Crystal Star]]'', a novel published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1994]]. She left the species unnamed, describing Lusa alternatively as a centauriform and a [[Wikipedia:Centaur|centaur]],<ref name="TCS" /> after the creature from Greek mythology.<ref name="WWNJO" /> Lusa was later used by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Rebecca Moesta]] in six books of their young adult series, the [[Star Wars: Young Jedi Knights|''Young Jedi Knights'']], published between [[1997]] and [[1998]]. Lusa's involvement in the Diversity Alliance was a key driver of the plot in that story arc, but she only made cameo appearances in the succeeding [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] story arc. Anderson and Moesta similarly left the species unnamed.

Lusa received an entry in [[1998]]'s ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', authored by [[Stephen J. Sansweet]], who added that Lusa was from the planet Chiron.<ref name="SWE" /> In Greek mythology, [[Wikipedia:Chiron|Chiron]] was the oldest and wisest of the centaurs.<ref>{{WebCite|url=https://www.theoi.com/Georgikos/KentaurosKheiron.html|text=CHIRON: The Wise Centaur|work=Theioi Greek Mythology|archivedate=20190819224815}}</ref> In [[2001]], Lusa's death was mentioned in [[Troy Denning]]'s ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'', part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series, and Denning was the first to identify the species as Chironians.<ref name="SBS8" /> This name has been reiterated in both the article "[[Who's Who in the New Jedi Order]]," published in [[Star Wars Insider 53|''Star Wars Insider'' 53]]—which also included the first, partial image of a Chironian, by [[Dan Veesenmeyer]]—and in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.
